flashcards.definition: The amount of heat needed to raise 1 g of a substance by 1°C (J/g·°C).
4.184 J/g·°C — very high, which moderates Earth's climate.
Heat = mass × specific heat × temperature change.
Absorbs a lot of heat with little temperature change (water, metals resist temp change less).
Solve for any variable: q (heat), m (mass), c (specific heat), or ΔT.
